name: Cherry Vinegar
recipeIngredient: 2 cups White wine vinegar or rice vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Puree the cherries with the vinegar in a bowl using an immersion blender or food processor until smooth.
2. Transfer the mixture to a stainless steel or enamel container.
3. Cover tightly and store at room temperature for 7 to 10 days, stirring occasionally to help release any trapped air bubbles.
4. After the desired fermentation period, strain the mixture through a coffee filter-lined sieve into another clean bowl.
5. Pour the strained vinegar into sterilized bottles.
6. Allow the vinegar to cool before refrigerating.
Makes approximately 2.5 cups
Use to marinate chicken or pork before grilling or BBQ, sprinkle over fruit or salads for an unusual flavor, or give as a natural hostess gift.
